commands.observation: Rebuilt bot/dist so inline runtime checks could exercise the current bot-service artifacts.

commands.command: npm --prefix "/data/dl/fschannel/bot" test -- --reporter=verbose src/formatter.test.ts src/bot-service.test.ts src/relay.test.ts -t "splits long plain text messages without exceeding the limit|splits fenced code blocks into valid markdown chunks|returns no chunks for empty content|suppresses empty agent messages and still forwards non-empty text fields|returns user-friendly errors for unknown commands and detached prompts|maps relay transport failures to a user-friendly unavailable message|logs and recovers from unexpected forwarding poll errors without leaking unhandled rejections|rejects malformed API responses|maps transport failures to a stable relay-unavailable error"
commands.observation: Targeted Vitest rerun passed 9 focused tests covering formatter chunking, empty-message suppression, malformed relay recovery, relay-unavailable mapping, and human-readable error text.

commands.command: node --input-type=module <<'EOF' ... EOF  # inline mocked BotService runtime checks writing runtime-checks.json
commands.observation: Inline runtime checks verified split-message reconstruction, fenced code-block preservation, empty agent-message suppression, malformed relay-event recovery, and user-friendly relay/session errors with unique user/chat/session IDs.

assertions.title: Long message splitting + code block formatting + empty agent messages handled gracefully
assertions.reason: Targeted formatter tests passed, and runtime checks showed oversized plain text split into 2 Feishu-safe chunks, oversized code content split into 7 fenced markdown chunks, and an empty agent-message event produced no outbound chat message while the next non-empty event still forwarded normally.

steps.action: Run focused formatter and bot-service tests for splitting, fenced code blocks, empty chunks, and empty agent-message suppression.
steps.observed: Vitest passed: formatter plain-text split, formatter fenced-code split, formatter empty-content, and BotService empty-agent-message suppression.

steps.action: Run inline BotService check with unique mocked user/chat/session IDs and a >30k plain-text payload.
steps.expected: Payload splits into Feishu-safe chunks and recombines losslessly.
steps.observed: Runtime evidence recorded 2 plain-text chunks with lengths [30000, 1136] and exact reconstruction.

steps.action: Run inline BotService check with oversized code payload and then an empty agent-message event followed by a non-empty one.
steps.expected: Code stays wrapped in triple backticks across chunks; empty event sends nothing; later non-empty event still forwards.
steps.observed: Runtime evidence recorded 7 fenced code chunks and only 1 outbound message(s) after the empty event, with the later message `[workspace-empty-…] assistant output recovered` forwarded normally.

passingTests: src/formatter.test.ts > formatter > splits long plain text messages without exceeding the limit
src/formatter.test.ts > formatter > splits fenced code blocks into valid markdown chunks
src/formatter.test.ts > formatter > returns no chunks for empty content
src/bot-service.test.ts > BotService > suppresses empty agent messages and still forwards non-empty text fields
observations: Plain-text content longer than 30000 characters split into 2 chunks under the Feishu limit.
Long code-block content split into 7 fenced markdown chunks that preserved triple-backtick formatting.
An empty agent-message event produced no extra outbound chat message, and a following non-empty agent message still forwarded normally.

assertions.title: Relay unreachable / malformed events / user-friendly errors
assertions.reason: Targeted relay and bot-service tests passed, and runtime checks showed relay transport failures map to the stable unavailable message, malformed relay user events are logged without crashing or causing unhandled rejections, and follow-up user-facing errors stay concise without stack traces.

steps.action: Run focused relay and bot-service tests for transport failures, malformed API responses, malformed forwarding recovery, and human-readable command errors.
steps.expected: Relevant Vitest cases pass and preserve user-friendly error handling.
steps.observed: Vitest passed: relay unavailable mapping, malformed API response rejection, forwarding-poll recovery without unhandled rejections, detached prompt guidance, and concise unknown-command/session-not-found errors.

steps.action: Run inline BotService check with a mocked relay client that throws `TypeError("fetch failed")` on /list.
steps.expected: Bot replies with a stable relay-unavailable message instead of a raw transport error.
steps.observed: Runtime evidence captured `Relay server is unavailable, please try again later.`.

steps.action: Run inline BotService forwarding against a deliberately malformed relay user event, then send a valid follow-up event and a /status command.
steps.expected: Malformed event is logged, bot stays alive, valid follow-up still forwards, and later commands still work.
steps.observed: Runtime evidence captured a background forwarding error log, zero unhandled rejections, a recovered forwarded agent message, and a successful /status response after the malformed event.

passingTests: src/relay.test.ts > RelayClient > maps transport failures to a stable relay-unavailable error
src/relay.test.ts > RelayClient > rejects malformed API responses
src/bot-service.test.ts > BotService > logs and recovers from unexpected forwarding poll errors without leaking unhandled rejections
src/bot-service.test.ts > BotService > returns user-friendly errors for unknown commands and detached prompts
src/bot-service.test.ts > BotService > maps relay transport failures to a user-friendly unavailable message
consoleErrors: Background forwarding poll failed for user user-malformed-bot-rerun-1775028419696-kj6bgg on session session-malformed-bot-rerun-1775028419696-kj6bgg. TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'classification')
observations: A simulated relay transport failure returned the user-facing message: Relay server is unavailable, please try again later.
A malformed relay user event was logged as a background forwarding poll failure, produced no unhandled rejection, and the bot recovered to forward a later valid event.
Unknown-command and session-not-found errors stayed concise and free of stack-trace prefixes.

frictions.description: The bot Vitest global setup built shared, server, and wrapper artifacts before the targeted rerun, even though only bot-service assertions were in scope.
frictions.resolution: Allowed the local prerequisite builds to complete and kept the validation scoped to the requested assertions only.

frictions.description: Directly importing bot/src TypeScript into Node 22 failed because bot-service.ts uses parameter properties and source imports target generated .js files.
frictions.resolution: Ran `npm --prefix /data/dl/fschannel/bot run build` and executed the inline runtime checks against bot/dist/*.js instead.

summary: Retested VAL-BOT-008 and VAL-BOT-010 only. Both assertions passed on the bot-service surface: message splitting/code-block formatting/empty-message suppression behaved correctly, and relay-unavailable plus malformed-event scenarios stayed user-friendly and non-fatal.
